what: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: R package working yield curves: fit smooth curves bond yields, extract forward rates discount factors, compute duration convexity, decompose curve movements.

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
code: install.packages("yieldcurves")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/yieldcurves") library(yieldcurves)  # US Treasury yields: maturities in years, rates as decimals (5% = 0.05) maturities <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 30) rates <- c(0.052, 0.050, 0.048, 0.045, 0.042, 0.040, 0.043)  fit <- yc_nelson_siegel(maturities, rates) fit #> -- Yield Curve (Nelson-Siegel) -- #> * Type: "zero" #> * Maturities: 7 (0.25Y to 30Y) #> * Rate range: 4% to 5.2% #> * RMSE: 8.6 bps #> * Parameters: beta0=0.04127, beta1=0.01347, beta2=-0.0091, tau=1  plot(fit)

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Government bond yields published daily handful maturities: 1-month, 3-month, 1-year, 2-year, 5-year, 10-year, 30-year. questions fixed income require yields maturities published, require derived quantities (forward rates, discount factors, risk measures) read table. need discount factor 7.5 years, implied rate 5-year loan starting 5 years, need fitted model. Nelson-Siegel (1987) Svensson (1994) models standard solution. fit entire curve 4 6 parameters economic meaning: level (long-run rate), slope (term premium), curvature (medium-term humps). 20 central banks use models official yield curve estimates. fitted curve, can extract forward rates, discount factors, carry roll-, duration convexity, Z-spreads. two existing R packages space. YieldCurve (last updated 2022) fits Nelson-Siegel Svensson models requires xts/zoo objects input, compute duration, convexity, Z-spreads, PCA, carry/roll-analysis. termstrc comprehensive (cubic splines, forward rates) archived CRAN 2018 due failing checks longer maintained. Neither package covers full workflow curve fitting risk measures portfolio analytics. yieldcurves fills gap: works plain numeric vectors, heavy dependencies, covers fitting, extraction, risk measures, decomposition one package.

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Nelson-Siegel model takes two inputs: maturities (years) rates (decimals, 5% = 0.05). returns fitted curve can query maturity.
code: library(yieldcurves)  maturities <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 20, 30) rates <- c(0.052, 0.050, 0.048, 0.045, 0.043, 0.042, 0.041,            0.040, 0.042, 0.043)  fit <- yc_nelson_siegel(maturities, rates) fit #> -- Yield Curve (Nelson-Siegel) -- #> * Type: "zero" #> * Maturities: 10 (0.25Y to 30Y) #> * Rate range: 4% to 5.2% #> * RMSE: 8.6 bps #> * Parameters: beta0=0.04127, beta1=0.01347, beta2=-0.0091, tau=1  plot(fit)

what: Extract forward rates and discount factors
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Forward rates tell market implies future interest rates. Discount factors tell future cash flow worth today.
code: # Forward rates at 1, 5, and 10 years yc_forward(fit, maturities = c(1, 5, 10)) #>   maturity forward_rate #> 1        1       0.0468 #> 2        5       0.0393 #> 3       10       0.0413  # Discount factors yc_discount(fit, maturities = c(1, 5, 10, 30)) #>   maturity discount_factor #> 1        1          0.9530 #> 2        5          0.8103 #> 3       10          0.6619 #> 4       30          0.2817   # $1 in 30 years is worth $0.28 today

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: much earn holding bond, assuming curve move? Carry yield income minus funding cost. Roll-capital gain bond’s remaining maturity shortens slides lower-rate part curve.

what: Compute duration and convexity for a coupon bond
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Duration measures sensitive bond’s price rate changes. modified duration 7.87 means 1% rate rise causes roughly 7.87% price drop.
code: # 10-year bond with 5% coupon at 4.5% yield, semi-annual coupons yc_bond_duration(face = 100, coupon_rate = 0.05, maturity = 10,                  yield = 0.045, frequency = 2) #> $macaulay_duration #> [1] 8.05 #> #> $modified_duration #> [1] 7.87 #> #> $convexity #> [1] 73.7 #> #> $price #> [1] 104.01

what: Compute the Z-spread on a bond
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Z-spread constant spread benchmark zero curve reprices bond market price. positive Z-spread means bond yields benchmark.
code: # Benchmark zero curve curve <- yc_curve(c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10), c(0.03, 0.035, 0.04, 0.042, 0.045))  # Bond trading below par result <- yc_zspread(price = 95, coupon_rate = 0.04, maturity = 5,                      curve = curve, frequency = 2) result$zspread #> [1] 0.0148   # 148 bps over the benchmark curve

what: Decompose yield curve movements with PCA
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Principal component analysis decomposes time series yield curves orthogonal factors. Litterman Scheinkman (1991) showed three factors (level, slope, curvature) explain 95% yield curve movements.
code: # Simulate a time series of yield curves (200 days, 5 tenors) set.seed(42) n_days <- 200 tenors <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 30) base <- c(0.045, 0.043, 0.042, 0.040, 0.043) level <- cumsum(rnorm(n_days, 0, 0.001)) curves <- matrix(NA, n_days, length(tenors)) for (i in seq_len(n_days)) curves[i, ] <- base + level[i] colnames(curves) <- paste0(tenors, "Y")  pca <- yc_pca(curves) pca #> -- Yield Curve PCA -- #> * Components: 3 #> * PC1 (Level): 97.2% variance #> * PC2 (Slope): 1.2% variance #> * PC3 (Curvature): 0.8% variance  plot(pca)

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: Zero rates (spot rates): yields zero-coupon bonds. building blocks yield curve. Par rates: coupon rate bond prices par (100). news means “10-year yield.” Forward rates: rates implied curve future period. “5y5y forward” rate market implies 5-year loan starting 5 years. Basis points (bps): 1 bp = 0.01%. rates package decimals: 5% = 0.05, 50 bps = 0.005.

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
text: supply two numeric vectors: maturities (years) rates (decimals, 5% = 0.05). can type directly pull data source.

title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
code: library(yieldcurves)  maturities <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5, 7, 10, 20, 30) rates <- c(0.052, 0.050, 0.048, 0.045, 0.043, 0.042, 0.041,            0.040, 0.042, 0.043)  fit <- yc_nelson_siegel(maturities, rates) plot(fit)

what: Option 2: Pull US Treasury yields from FRED
title: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition
code: # 1. Install the fred package (one time) install.packages("fred")  # 2. Get a free API key from https://fred.stlouisfed.org/docs/api/api_key.html fred::fred_set_key("your_api_key_here")  # 3. Download Treasury constant maturity rates library(fred) treasury <- fred_series(c("DGS1", "DGS2", "DGS5", "DGS10", "DGS30"))  # 4. Extract the most recent observation latest <- treasury[nrow(treasury), ] maturities <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 30) rates <- as.numeric(latest[, -1]) / 100  # FRED reports percent, convert to decimal  # 5. Fit a yield curve library(yieldcurves) fit <- yc_nelson_siegel(maturities, rates) plot(fit)

Estimating Interpreting Forward Interest Rates: Sweden 1992-1994. NBER Working Paper, 4871. doi:10.3386/w4871 Litterman, R. Scheinkman, J. (1991). Common Factors Affecting Bond Returns. Journal Fixed Income, 1(1), 54-61. doi:10.3905/jfi.1991.692347

what: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
title: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
text: Compute Macaulay duration, modified duration, convexity coupon-bearing bond.

title: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
code: yc_bond_duration(   face = 100,   coupon_rate,   maturity,   yield,   frequency = 2,   compounding = c("semi_annual", "annual", "continuous") )

title: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
text: face Numeric. Face (par) value bond. Default 100. coupon_rate Numeric. Annual coupon rate decimal (e.g., 0.05 5 percent). maturity Numeric. Time maturity years. yield Numeric. Yield maturity decimal. frequency Integer. Coupon frequency per year: 1 annual 2 semi-annual (default). compounding Character. Compounding convention: "semi_annual" (default), "annual", "continuous".

title: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
text: list components macaulay_duration, modified_duration, convexity, price.

title: Coupon Bond Duration and Convexity — yc_bond_duration
code: # 2-year 5% bond at 4% yield, semi-annual coupons yc_bond_duration(face = 100, coupon_rate = 0.05, maturity = 2,                  yield = 0.04, frequency = 2) #> $macaulay_duration #> [1] 1.928783 #>  #> $modified_duration #> [1] 1.890964 #>  #> $convexity #> [1] 4.578047 #>  #> $price #> [1] 101.9039 #>

title: Carry and Roll-Down Analysis — yc_carry
text: Decompose expected return holding bond carry (yield income minus financing cost) roll-(capital gain sliding curve).

title: Carry and Roll-Down Analysis — yc_carry
code: yc_carry(curve, maturities = NULL, horizon = 1/12, funding_rate = NULL)

title: Carry and Roll-Down Analysis — yc_carry
text: curve yc_curve object. maturities Numeric vector bond maturities analyse. NULL, uses curve's maturities (excluding shortest). horizon Numeric. Holding period years. Default 1/12 (one month). funding_rate Optional numeric. Overnight funding rate decimal. NULL, uses shortest rate curve.

title: Carry and Roll-Down Analysis — yc_carry
text: data frame columns maturity, carry, rolldown, total.

title: Carry and Roll-Down Analysis — yc_carry

what: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline
title: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline

title: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline
code: yc_cubic_spline(   maturities,   rates,   method = c("natural", "fmm"),   type = c("zero", "par", "forward"),   date = NULL )

title: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years. rates Numeric vector observed yields decimals. method Character. Spline method: "natural" (default) "fmm" (Forsythe, Malcolm, Moler). type Character. Rate type: "zero" (default), "par", "forward". date Optional Date curve.

title: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline
text: yc_curve object method = "cubic_spline".

title: Fit Cubic Spline Yield Curve — yc_cubic_spline

what: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
title: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
text: Construct yc_curve object observed maturity-rate pairs. core data structure used throughout package.

title: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
code: yc_curve(maturities, rates, type = c("zero", "par", "forward"), date = NULL)

title: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years (e.g., 0.25 3 months, 2 2 years). rates Numeric vector yields decimals (e.g., 0.05 5\ Must length maturities. type Character. type rate: "zero" (default), "par", "forward". date Optional Date curve observation.

title: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
text: yc_curve object (S3 class) components: maturities Numeric vector maturities years. rates Numeric vector rates decimals. type Character string indicating rate type. method Character string indicating fitting method. params List model parameters (empty observed curves). fitted Numeric vector fitted rates (NULL observed curves). residuals Numeric vector residuals (NULL observed curves). date Date curve observation. n_obs Integer count maturity points.

title: Create a Yield Curve Object — yc_curve
code: # US Treasury yields (2Y, 5Y, 10Y, 30Y) maturities <- c(2, 5, 10, 30) rates <- c(0.045, 0.042, 0.040, 0.043) curve <- yc_curve(maturities, rates) curve #>  #> ── Yield Curve (Observed) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: "zero" #> • Maturities: 4 (2Y to 30Y) #> • Rate range: 4% to 4.5%

title: Compute Discount Factors — yc_discount
text: Calculate discount factors yield curve assuming continuous compounding.

title: Compute Discount Factors — yc_discount
code: yc_discount(   curve,   maturities = NULL,   compounding = c("continuous", "annual", "semi_annual") )

title: Compute Discount Factors — yc_discount
text: curve yc_curve object. maturities Optional numeric vector maturities. NULL, uses curve's maturities. compounding Character. Compounding convention: "continuous" (default), "annual", "semi_annual".

title: Compute Discount Factors — yc_discount
text: data frame columns maturity discount_factor.

title: Compute Discount Factors — yc_discount
code: maturities <- c(1, 2, 5, 10) rates <- c(0.045, 0.043, 0.042, 0.040) curve <- yc_curve(maturities, rates) yc_discount(curve) #>   maturity discount_factor #> 1        1       0.9559975 #> 2        2       0.9175942 #> 3        5       0.8105842 #> 4       10       0.6703200 yc_discount(curve, compounding = "annual") #>   maturity discount_factor #> 1        1       0.9569378 #> 2        2       0.9192452 #> 3        5       0.8140694 #> 4       10       0.6755642

title: Duration and Convexity — yc_duration
text: Compute Macaulay duration, modified duration, convexity zero-coupon bonds maturity curve.

title: Duration and Convexity — yc_duration
code: yc_duration(   curve,   maturities = NULL,   compounding = c("continuous", "annual", "semi_annual") )

title: Duration and Convexity — yc_duration
text: curve yc_curve object. maturities Optional numeric vector maturities. NULL, uses curve's maturities. compounding Character. Compounding convention: "continuous" (default), "annual", "semi_annual".

title: Duration and Convexity — yc_duration
text: data frame columns maturity, macaulay_duration, modified_duration, convexity.

title: Duration and Convexity — yc_duration
Fit a Yield Curve — yc_fit
text: Unified interface fitting yield curve using different methods. Dispatches yc_nelson_siegel(), yc_svensson(), yc_cubic_spline().

title: Fit a Yield Curve — yc_fit
code: yc_fit(   maturities,   rates,   method = c("nelson_siegel", "svensson", "cubic_spline"),   type = c("zero", "par", "forward"),   date = NULL,   ... )

title: Fit a Yield Curve — yc_fit
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years. rates Numeric vector observed yields decimals. method Character. Fitting method: "nelson_siegel" (default), "svensson", "cubic_spline". type Character. Rate type: "zero" (default), "par", "forward". date Optional Date curve. ... Additional arguments passed fitting function.

title: Fit a Yield Curve — yc_fit
text: yc_curve object.

title: Fit a Yield Curve — yc_fit
code: maturities <- c(0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10, 30) rates <- c(0.052, 0.050, 0.048, 0.045, 0.042, 0.040, 0.043) fit <- yc_fit(maturities, rates, method = "nelson_siegel") fit #>  #> ── Yield Curve (Nelson-Siegel) ───────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Type: "zero" #> • Maturities: 7 (0.25Y to 30Y) #> • Rate range: 4% to 5.2% #> • RMSE: "4.7" bps #> • Parameters: beta0=0.04444, beta1=0.00837, beta2=-0.02454, tau=3

title: Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward
text: Compute forward rates yield curve. Can compute either instantaneous forward rates forward-forward rates two tenors.

title: Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward
Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward
text: curve yc_curve object. maturities Optional numeric vector maturities compute forward rates. NULL, uses curve's maturities. horizon Optional numeric. provided, computes forward rate maturity maturity + horizon (forward-forward rate).

title: Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward
text: data frame columns maturity forward_rate.

title: Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward
text: instantaneous forward rate derived : $$f(m) = r(m) + m \cdot r'(m)$$

title: Extract Forward Rates — yc_forward

text: data frame columns maturity rate.

title: Interpolate Yield Curve — yc_interpolate
Key Rate Durations — yc_key_rate_duration
text: Compute key rate durations bumping yield curve specific tenors. bump triangular: full shift applied key rate tenor linearly interpolated zero adjacent key rate tenors.

title: Key Rate Durations — yc_key_rate_duration
code: yc_key_rate_duration(   coupon_rate,   maturity,   curve,   key_rates = c(1, 2, 5, 10, 30),   shift = 1e-04,   face = 100,   frequency = 2 )

title: Key Rate Durations — yc_key_rate_duration
text: coupon_rate Numeric. Annual coupon rate decimal. maturity Numeric. Time maturity years. curve Either yc_curve object list/data frame components maturities rates. key_rates Numeric vector key rate tenors years. Default c(1, 2, 5, 10, 30). shift Numeric. Size rate bump decimal (default 0.0001, .e. 1 basis point). face Numeric. Face value. Default 100. frequency Integer. Coupon frequency: 1 (annual) 2 (semi-annual, default).

title: Key Rate Durations — yc_key_rate_duration
text: data frame columns tenor key_rate_duration.

title: Key Rate Durations — yc_key_rate_duration

what: Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
title: Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
text: Nelson-Siegel Svensson curves, extracts estimated factors directly model parameters. curves, computes empirical measures.

title: Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
text: curve yc_curve object.

title: Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
text: named list : level Long-run level (beta0 NS/Svensson, mean rate). slope Slope factor (beta1 NS/Svensson, short - long rate). curvature Curvature factor (beta2 NS/Svensson, 2*mid - short - long rate).

title: Extract Level, Slope, and Curvature Factors — yc_level_slope_curvature
Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
text: Estimate Nelson-Siegel (1987) yield curve model observed maturity-rate pairs. model decomposes yield curve three factors: level, slope, curvature.

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
code: yc_nelson_siegel(   maturities,   rates,   tau_init = 1,   weights = NULL,   type = c("zero", "par", "forward"),   date = NULL )

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years. rates Numeric vector observed yields decimals. tau_init Numeric. Initial value decay parameter tau. Default 1. weights Optional numeric vector weights observation. Must length maturities. Useful emphasising liquid tenors. NULL (default), observations equally weighted. type Character. Rate type: "zero" (default), "par", "forward". date Optional Date curve.

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
text: yc_curve object method = "nelson_siegel" params containing beta0, beta1, beta2, tau.

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
text: Nelson-Siegel model : $$r(m) = \beta_0 + \beta_1 \frac{1 - e^{-m/\tau}}{m/\tau} + \beta_2 \left(\frac{1 - e^{-m/\tau}}{m/\tau} - e^{-m/\tau}\right)$$

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel
text: Nelson, C.R. Siegel, .F. (1987). Parsimonious Modeling Yield Curves. Journal Business, 60(4), 473–489. doi:10.1086/296409

title: Fit Nelson-Siegel Yield Curve — yc_nelson_siegel

title: Convert Par Rates to Zero Rates — yc_par_to_zero
Convert Par Rates to Zero Rates — yc_par_to_zero
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years (must positive integers half-years). par_rates Numeric vector par rates decimals. frequency Integer. Coupon frequency per year: 1 annual (default) 2 semi-annual.

title: Convert Par Rates to Zero Rates — yc_par_to_zero
text: data frame columns maturity zero_rate.

title: Convert Par Rates to Zero Rates — yc_par_to_zero

what: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
title: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
text: Perform PCA time series yield curves extract dominant factors (level, slope, curvature) following Litterman Scheinkman (1991).

title: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
text: curves_matrix Numeric matrix row yield curve observation (e.g., daily curves) column tenor. Column names maturity labels. n_components Integer. Number principal components retain. Default 3 (level, slope, curvature). scale Logical. Whether scale variables PCA. Default FALSE (use covariance matrix, standard yield curve PCA).

title: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
text: yc_pca object (S3 class) components: loadings Matrix factor loadings (tenors x components). scores Matrix factor scores (observations x components). variance_explained Numeric vector proportion variance explained component. cumulative_variance Numeric vector cumulative variance explained. sdev Standard deviations component. n_components Number components retained. tenors Column names input matrix.

title: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
text: Litterman, R. Scheinkman, J. (1991). Common Factors Affecting Bond Returns. Journal Fixed Income, 1(1), 54–61. doi:10.3905/jfi.1991.692347

title: Principal Component Analysis of Yield Curves — yc_pca
code: # Simulate 100 days of yield curves at 5 tenors set.seed(42) n_days <- 100 tenors <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 30) base_rates <- c(0.045, 0.043, 0.042, 0.040, 0.043) curves <- matrix(NA, n_days, length(tenors)) colnames(curves) <- paste0(tenors, "Y") level <- cumsum(rnorm(n_days, 0, 0.001)) slope <- cumsum(rnorm(n_days, 0, 0.0005)) for (i in seq_len(n_days)) {   curves[i, ] <- base_rates + level[i] + slope[i] * (tenors - mean(tenors)) / 30 } pca_result <- yc_pca(curves) pca_result #>  #> ── Yield Curve PCA ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Components: 3 #> • Tenors: 5 (1Y, 2Y, 5Y, 10Y, 30Y) #> • PC1 (Level): 93.3% variance #> • PC2 (Slope): 6.7% variance #> • PC3 (Curvature): 0% variance #> • Cumulative: 100%

Yield Curve Slope Measures — yc_slope

title: Yield Curve Slope Measures — yc_slope
text: named list slope measures: spread_2s10s 10-year minus 2-year rate (common slope measure). spread_2s30s 30-year minus 2-year rate. spread_5s30s 30-year minus 5-year rate. spread_3m10y 10-year minus 3-month rate (term premium proxy). butterfly_2s5s10s 2 * 5-year minus 2-year minus 10-year (curvature measure). Returns NA measure whose required tenors fall outside curve range.

title: Yield Curve Slope Measures — yc_slope
Fit Svensson Yield Curve — yc_svensson
text: Estimate Svensson (1994) yield curve model observed maturity-rate pairs. Extends Nelson-Siegel adding second curvature term decay parameter, providing greater flexibility curves two humps.

title: Fit Svensson Yield Curve — yc_svensson
code: yc_svensson(   maturities,   rates,   tau1_init = 1,   tau2_init = 5,   weights = NULL,   type = c("zero", "par", "forward"),   date = NULL )

title: Fit Svensson Yield Curve — yc_svensson
text: maturities Numeric vector maturities years. rates Numeric vector observed yields decimals. tau1_init Numeric. Initial value first decay parameter. Default 1. tau2_init Numeric. Initial value second decay parameter. Default 5. weights Optional numeric vector weights observation. Must length maturities. NULL (default), observations equally weighted. type Character. Rate type: "zero" (default), "par", "forward". date Optional Date curve.

title: Fit Svensson Yield Curve — yc_svensson
text: yc_curve object method = "svensson" params containing beta0, beta1, beta2, beta3, tau1, tau2.

title: Fit Svensson Yield Curve — yc_svensson
text: Svensson, L.E.O. (1994). Estimating Interpreting Forward Interest Rates: Sweden 1992–1994. NBER Working Paper, 4871. doi:10.3386/w4871

Z-Spread — yc_zspread
text: Compute Z-spread (zero-volatility spread) bond. Z-spread constant spread added zero rate benchmark curve makes discounted cash flows equal market price.

title: Z-Spread — yc_zspread
code: yc_zspread(price, coupon_rate, maturity, curve, face = 100, frequency = 2)

title: Z-Spread — yc_zspread
text: price Numeric. Market price bond. coupon_rate Numeric. Annual coupon rate decimal. maturity Numeric. Time maturity years. curve Either yc_curve object list/data frame components maturities rates. face Numeric. Face value bond. Default 100. frequency Integer. Coupon frequency per year: 1 annual 2 semi-annual (default).

title: Z-Spread — yc_zspread
text: list components zspread (Z-spread decimal), price (input price), model_price (price implied curve Z-spread applied).

title: Z-Spread — yc_zspread
code: # Create a benchmark curve curve <- yc_curve(c(0.5, 1, 2, 5, 10), c(0.03, 0.035, 0.04, 0.042, 0.045))  # A bond priced below par (positive Z-spread) yc_zspread(price = 95, coupon_rate = 0.04, maturity = 5,            curve = curve, frequency = 2) #> $zspread #> [1] 0.01029722 #>  #> $price #> [1] 95 #>  #> $model_price #> [1] 95 #>

what: yieldcurves: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition — yieldcurves-package
title: yieldcurves: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition — yieldcurves-package
text: Fits yield curves using Nelson-Siegel (1987) doi:10.1086/296409 , Svensson (1994) doi:10.3386/w4871 , cubic spline methods. Extracts forward rates, discount factors, par rates fitted curves. Computes duration convexity risk measures. Computes Z-spread key rate durations. Provides principal component decomposition following Litterman Scheinkman (1991) doi:10.3905/jfi.1991.692347 , carry roll-analysis, slope measures. methods pure computation external dependencies beyond base R; works yield data source.

title: yieldcurves: Yield Curve Fitting, Analysis, and Decomposition — yieldcurves-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

title: yieldcurves 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-26 Initial release. Nelson-Siegel (1987) Svensson (1994) yield curve fitting multi-start optimization optional observation weights. Cubic spline interpolation via stats::splinefun. Forward rate extraction (analytical NS/Svensson, numerical splines). Discount factor computation continuous, annual, semi-annual compounding. Duration convexity zero-coupon bonds via yc_duration(). Coupon bond duration, modified duration, convexity via yc_bond_duration() (annual, semi-annual, continuous compounding). Z-spread computation via yc_zspread(). Key rate durations via yc_key_rate_duration(). Par--zero zero--par rate conversions via bootstrap stripping, supporting annual semi-annual coupon frequencies. Principal component decomposition yield curve time series. Carry roll-analysis. Slope measures (2s10s, 2s30s, butterfly) level-slope-curvature decomposition.
